sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The click on N(W) in the left container did NOT change the N token colors. The before and after states are identical for the N tokens. This suggests that clicking on an N token inside a container doesn't swap colors, or the container "locks" the center color.
Let me reconsider the goal. Looking at the image:
- Left container has N(W) inside (green border, white center)
- N(c) is at cols 30-32 (green border, charcoal center)
- Right container is empty (has charcoal border, white interior)
Maybe the goal is to have:
- N(c) in the left container (charcoal center matches charcoal border)
- N(W) in the right container (white center matches white interior)
But I can't click on N(W) in the container to swap colors. So maybe I need to:
1. Move N(W) out of the left container
2. Click on N(W) to swap colors (making it N(c))
3. Move N(c) back into the left container
Or maybe the goal is simpler: just have N(c) in the left container. Let me try:
1. Move N(W) out of the left container (UP)
2. Click on N(W) to swap colors
3. Move N(c) back into the left container (DOWN)
Wait, but when I clicked on N(W) earlier (when it was at rows 24-26, cols 21-23), the colors DID swap. So clicking works outside containers. Let me try this sequence.
Actually, let me first check: is N(W) in the left container the active token? If so, I can move it UP to get it out, then click to swap, then move DOWN to put N(c) in.
Let me try: UP (to move N(W) out of container), MOUSE (to swap), DOWN (to move N(c) back in).

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Current state:
# N(c) at rows 30-32, cols 30-32
# N(W) at rows 33-35, cols 12-14 (in left container)

# The click on N(W) in container didn't swap colors
# Hypothesis: Clicking only works outside containers
# Strategy: Move N(W) out, click to swap, move N(c) back in

# N(W) is at rows 33-35, cols 12-14
# Move UP to get out of container: rows 30-32, cols 12-14
# Then click to swap colors: N(W) becomes N(c), N(c) becomes N(W)
# Then move DOWN to put N(c) in container: rows 33-35, cols 12-14

action(['UP', {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 31, 'col': 13}, 'DOWN'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
